attacking_style: Opportunistic rather than explosive; gains GC positions through breakaway placements and steady climbing rather than late-race accelerations. Rarely initiates proactive attacks when in defensive positions.
team_tactics: Trek demonstrates conservative resource management, avoiding aggressive chasing early in stage races. Limited domestique support forces Lopez to mark threats personally, even when holding leader's jersey.
terrain_preference: Sustained, high-altitude climbing (4000m+ days) where steady tempo suits him. Exposed on nervous flat stages and technical finales requiring positional awareness and split reactions.
vulnerability: Fades over multi-week duration; loses form in third week of Grand Tours. Vulnerable in chaotic flat racing where positioning and quick reactions to splits are critical, not pure climbing power.
key_patterns: Loses GC position through flat-stage splits and positioning errors, not mountain weakness (2 instances).
Shows unexpected resilience defending lead initially but cannot sustain through week three (4 observations).
Relies on personal marking rather than team protection even in leader's jersey (2 instances).
Passive on final climbs when not defending; hangs but doesn't attack proactively (2 observations).
Exceeded pre-race GC expectations initially but form trajectory consistently negative over three weeks.
form_trajectory: negative: 2022 Giro showed initial resilience holding pink for 10 days but faded to 9th. 2023 Vuelta appearance shows continued passive climbing with no proactive threat signals.
